niche: economics, markets, policy, trade, current trends

voice.engineLicense: MIT (piper binary/voices, per repo License panel; note active dev moved to OHF-Voice/piper1-gpl which is GPL-3.0 -- this channel pins the MIT-licensed classic piper artifact per its own research)
voice.voiceLicenseNote: Dataset-level license for the ryan checkpoint specifically was not independently re-verified this pass (per research doc Open Gaps) -- verify huggingface.co/rhasspy/piper-voices/tree/main/en/en_US/ryan before shipping.
voice.note: Standard American male voice, medium-quality tier -- reads as neutral/authoritative, the channel's own research doc's stated fit for a sharp/analytical macro-explainer register (vs. warmer voices like amy/kristin better suited to lifestyle-tone channels).

music.note: Copyright Risk Flag in research doc (trending-track discovery upside vs. Content ID/takedown risk on a monetized, automated channel) is an explicit operator decision, not resolved here. Pilot defaults to the safe (non-trending) path, same as Mind Mosaic and Penny Blueprint -- see pipeline music-bed generation for what is actually wired vs. still a stub.

sfx.source: @rse/soundfx (CC-0 / CC-BY-3.0), individual soundfx.d/*.mp3 files vendored locally

visualTokens.notes: background/calmInsight/lightnessEnergy/captionEmphasis are all directly PIL-sampled hex values from the downloaded reference clip (research/visual-reference/market-lens.md Extracted Color Tokens): near-black chart backdrop (#000000-#010000, used as #050505 to keep a hint of lift for a text-only canvas), chart-grid accent blue (#265DBA), US-flag blue (#3369BC), and the opening bumper's purple gradient (#58347A -> #815EA9). tensionBias (#C4432E) is NOT a measured token -- the moodboard explicitly flags a red 'up-arrow' accent as 'observed, not hex-confirmed' and instructs not to treat any specific red hex as sourced. This value is a deliberate, disclosed synthesis (a standard alert-red) standing in for that unconfirmed accent, same disclosure pattern as Mind Mosaic's blue-named-but-not-swatched note.

typography.captionStyle: bold, heavily condensed sans-serif, slight italic slant, all-caps for bumper/chart-overlay text
typography.captionRevealUnit: additive stacking for checklist-style reveals; stat callouts pop/settle rather than fade
typography.emphasisTier: single vivid chart-accent blue (#265DBA) emphasis tone against the near-black canvas, echoing the reference clip's own on-screen chart/stat-callout color

spacing.infographicReveal: staggered/incremental per DataChart/InfoCard, matching the reference's additive checklist-build pattern
spacing.brollDensity: allow a headline stat plus one supporting micro-label, cap concurrent on-screen text blocks at ~3 per moodboard Motion-Personality Direction

pacing.note: Reference clip's fast section runs a new overlay/cut roughly every 3s (measured via 3s-interval sampling, see moodboard Motion/Pacing Notes) before relaxing to 9-15s holds during the checklist build-up. Adapted here as beat duration driven by VO length (per existing pipeline pattern) rather than a fixed cut clock.

motionPersonality.easingNote: spring/overshoot easing IS appropriate here, per the moodboard's directly-measured 'pop in, settle' checkmark/stat-callout reveal behavior -- this channel's DataChart beats use spring-based bar growth (see tokens.chartMotion = energetic) rather than the plain ease-out-cubic every other channel in this pipeline uses.
